purpose: SIGGRAPH paper demo candidates for text-to-Genesis generation and tuning.
selection_rules: No cloth, fluid, or RL/control-heavy examples.
Prefer complete paper demo scenes over isolated stress tests.
Keep the suite balanced across pure rigid, soft with rigid boundaries, and rigid-soft two-way coupling.

examples.title: anchor_chain_windup
examples.prompt: @layout layouts/anchor_chain_windup.json Create a source-layout-guided pure rigid-body IPC contact scene with no ground against a pure white background: recreate the SIGGRAPH 2021 Rigid IPC anchor-chain windup using the supplied layout as authoritative geometry and initial-placement guidance. Reuse the layout-provided ready repo_asset meshes for the spool, chain link, and anchor instead of regenerating approximations when those manifest entries are ready. Use a gray fixed-position motorized rolling axle/spool above a hanging purple interlocking chain and a heavy gold anchor load at the bottom. The chain must not be a rope, tube, curve, cable, tendon, or single fused mesh: instantiate the layout's independent rigid chain-link mesh bodies, preserving the source count unless an explicitly documented Genesis scaling adaptation is needed. Each link should match the source link structure: one continuous closed elongated torus-topology ring with a real open slot, rounded ends, thick tube cross-section, and 90-degree alternating neighboring orientations so adjacent links physically pass through one another. Let the chain and anchor briefly release and fall under gravity, then rotate only the axle/spool so the individual links wind upward, collide, interlock, bunch, kink, and lift/swing the anchor through contact. Continue rotating after reel-up so the bunched chain transfers angular momentum and flings the anchor outward; the anchor must be thrown by chain tension/contact, not by teleporting, hidden constraints, pose writes, or direct anchor forces. Render fps has been set 0.25x real time, so render 15s real time / 60s video time / 1500 simulation (render) steps.
examples.notes: A complete rigid chain-and-winch demo; no deformable bodies, fluids, cloth, or learned control.

examples.title: bike_chain_sprocket
examples.prompt: Create a pure rigid-body scene with a loop of interlocking chain links around two toothed sprockets; motorize one sprocket so the chain wraps, slides, and drives the other sprocket through tight contact, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A contact-rich rigid mechanism demo with a simple motorized sprocket control.

examples.title: card_house_topple
examples.prompt: Create a pure rigid-body IPC scene of a delicate card-house collapse on a gray platform. Build an A-frame tower from 10-14 separate thin rigid rectangular planks in red, blue, tan, and dark gray, leaning in pairs with small contact gaps and cross bars. Drop two or three small rigid cubes onto the top so the tower buckles, planks slide, tip, collide, and settle into a messy pile. Treat every card as a rigid mesh with thickness and IPC contact; do not use cloth sheets or kinematic collapse animation; render 8s behavior.
examples.notes: Pure rigid codimensional-looking demo adapted to finite-thickness rigid planks.

examples.title: chainmail_net_ball_drop
examples.prompt: Create a pure rigid-body IPC contact scene on a pure white background: a large dark-blue rigid ball drops into a suspended square chainmail net or basket made from many independent small pale-gold oval torus links. The net must not be cloth, a sheet, or a fused mesh; instantiate a grid of separate rigid ring links with real holes, each interlocked with neighboring rings so the net can sag through contact. Hold or anchor only the four outer corners/edges, then release the ball above the center. The ball should push the linked rings downward, make the net bowl deeply, cause local rings to rotate, bunch, and tighten around the ball, then settle while all motion comes from rigid IPC contact and gravity. Render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: Anchor-like many-link rigid contact scene; approximates the Rigid IPC chainmail net as a finite grid of independent torus links, not cloth.

examples.title: expanding_lock_box
examples.prompt: Create a pure rigid-body mechanism scene of an expanding lock box: a gear-like key turns a central spiral cam, retracts five sliding locking pins through contact, then the bottom panel drops open under gravity, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A complete rigid mechanical demo and a good long-term target for generated articulated/mesh assets.

examples.title: halloween_pumpkin_party
examples.paper: Penetration-free Projective Dynamics on the GPU
examples.prompt: Create a stylized soft-body Halloween scene with soft pumpkin and ghost toy meshes bouncing around a rigid twisted tree and ground props, colliding and settling with no fabric or fluids, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A complete stylized soft-body scene. It relies on generated toy/prop meshes but no cloth or fluid simulation.

examples.title: soft_dragon_obstacle_course
examples.paper: Penetration-free Projective Dynamics on the GPU
examples.prompt: Create a soft-body scene where a long soft dragon toy is released through a field of fixed vertical rigid posts, bending, twisting, and tumbling around the obstacles through contact, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A non-cloth soft mesh demo with static rigid obstacle contact.

examples.title: soft_toys_squeeze_through_tube
examples.paper: Incremental Potential Contact: Intersection- and Inversion-Free, Large-Deformation Dynamics
examples.prompt: Create a rigid-soft coupled squeeze-out scene inside a fixed transparent mesh container whose upper part is a vertical cylindrical tube and whose lower part is a tapered conical funnel with an extremely tiny flat-cut circular opening (much smaller than the following objects) at the very bottom. Pack the container with the following medium-hard deformable FEM toy meshes with top-down sequence: a heavy red soft ball, a crab toy, a dinosaur, a squatting lion, and an alien-shaped toy. The size of these meshes should be closed to but a little bit smaller than the top radius of the container. All meshes above drops downward and visibly squashes, bulges, jams against each other and the transparent wall, partly squeezes through the small bottom opening, rebounds, but keep inside the container without fully dropping down. Render fps has been set 0.25x real time, so render 7.5s real time / 30s video time / 750 simulation (render) steps.
examples.notes: A complete IPC squeeze-out demo adapted to two-way coupling by making the plunger a physical rigid participant.

examples.title: spiky_soft_balls_pinball
examples.paper: Second-Order Stencil Descent for Interior-Point Hyperelasticity
examples.prompt: Create a soft-body pinball-like scene with several spiky soft balls inside a narrow arena of fixed rigid side walls and staggered short rigid pegs; launch or drop the balls so they bounce, snag, deform, and settle around the pegs, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A non-cloth soft-body contact scene with repeated obstacle impacts.

examples.title: woven_basket_soft_impact
examples.paper: Second-Order Stencil Descent for Interior-Point Hyperelasticity
examples.prompt: Create a rigid-soft coupled scene where a deformable woven basket made from elastic soft ring or lattice elements supports rigid branch-like objects and soft foliage; the objects fall into the basket, deform it deeply, and settle together through two-way contact, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A more complete mixed rigid/deformable demo without fabric. Approximate the woven structure with generated soft lattice or soft torus/ring elements.

examples.title: wrecking_ball_block_pile
examples.prompt: Create a pure rigid-body IPC contact scene on a white background with a gray platform, a compact stack or pile of many small pink rigid cubes, and a heavy gold wrecking ball hanging from a short chain of separate dark metal oval links. The chain should be made from individual interlocked rigid mesh rings, not a rope or curve. Release the ball from the side so it swings under gravity into the cube pile, punches through the stack, scatters dozens of cubes outward, then swings back while cubes collide, tumble, and settle on the platform. Use only rigid IPC contact/friction and gravity; do not teleport the ball or cubes; render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: Combines the anchor-style chain with card-house-style many-rigid-body collapse; no soft bodies, cloth, fluid, or learned control.
